title: Service Unit Operators, Oil and Gas
description: Operate equipment to increase oil flow from producing wells or to remove stuck pipe, casing, tools, or other obstructions from drilling wells. Includes fishing-tool technicians.

tasks.text: Maintain and perform safety inspections on equipment and tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate controls that raise derricks or level rigs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Listen to engines, rotary chains, or other equipment to detect faulty operations or unusual well conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare reports of services rendered, tools used, or time required, for billing purposes.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with others to gather information regarding pipe or tool sizes or borehole conditions in wells.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate pumps that circulate water, oil, or other fluids through wells to remove sand or other materials obstructing the free flow of oil.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive truck-mounted units to well sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret instrument readings to ascertain the depth of obstruction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Thread cables through derrick pulleys, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select fishing methods or tools for removing obstacles such as liners, broken casing, screens, or drill pipe.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Close and seal wells no longer in use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct drilling crews performing activities such as assembling and connecting pipe, applying weights to drill pipes, or drilling around lodged obstacles.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply green technologies or techniques, such as the use of coiled tubing, slim-hole drilling, horizontal drilling, hydraulic fracturing, or gas lift systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate specialized equipment to remove obstructions by backing off or severing pipes by chemical or explosive action.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perforate well casings or sidewalls of boreholes with explosive charges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine unserviceable wells to determine actions to be taken to improve well conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor sound wave-generating or detecting mechanisms to determine well fluid levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Insert detection instruments into wells with obstructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
